calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF PERFORMANCE FOOD GROUP, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion for an order assigning rights, restraining order, and turnover order is denied without prejudice.

Judgment debtor Performance Food Group Inc. obtained a default judgment against the Napa Deli, Inc. et al. on July 11, 2025. Based on the complaint and the declaration of Cristin Garden submitted for the prove-up hearing, of which the court takes judicial notice, this action appears to concern unpaid food bills, not dental bills. Yet Performance Food's attorney declaration recites that the defendant is a dental practice. (Amended Dec. of David J. Cook, para. 2.) That appears to be in error. The list of persons and entities from whom judgment debtor seeks an assignment error is mostly a list of insurance companies, including many dental insurance companies, who seem unlikely to this court to be payees of this judgment creditor.

Code of Civil Procedure 708.510(a) makes clear that the assignment remedy is discretionary by stating that it is a remedy the court "may" order. On this application, the court declines to issue the remedy on the record before it because there is no proof that judgment debtor has demanded payment of the judgment, and because the list of likely payees is insufficiently supported. This order is without prejudice.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 585(A)(&)(B)
Off Calendar Jul-11-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
This matter was set for Prove- Up for a Request for Default Judgment against Defendant The Napa Deli, Inc. A Corporation, a.k.a. The Napa Deli and Defendant David Barraza, An Individual.

Dept: 505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K.Valdes; Bailiff: Deputy Guitron; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" commencing at 9:30:03 a.m. and concluding at 9:51:52 a.m.(D505)

David Cook, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff Performance Food Group, Nc. A Corporation.
Defendant The Napa Deli, Inc. A Corporation, a.k.a. The Napa Deli and Defendant David Barraza, An Individual not appearing.
Cristin Garden, sworn to testify for the Plaintiff.

Plaintiff's complaint filed Oct. 15, 2024.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendants filed Oct. 22, 2024.
Dismissal of Does filed on Mar. 11, 2025.
Default entered as to Defendants on Mar. 11, 2025.

The Court examines the witness. Witness testifies as to the declarations filed and proof to the request for damages.

Counsel states that declarations and statements made are true and correct.
The court denies the request for pre-judgment interest and does not find enough evidence to support the request.

Good cause appearing, the Court finds that the Plaintiff has proven its prima facie case and rules in favor of the Plaintiff against the Defendants as follows:

The Plaintiff shall recover from the Defendants:
Damages $19,856.78
Pre-Judgment Interest at 10% $0
Costs $0
Attorney's Fees $500.00
Other $0
Total Judgment $20,356.78

Proposed judgment as modified signed and executed this day.

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 585(A)(&)(B)
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Continued To Jul-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Deputies Gasper and Brown; Dept. 505
Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:56 a.m. and concluding at 11:03 a.m.

Plaintiff Performance Food Group appearing represented by David Julian Cook, Esq., testifies.
Witness for the Plaintiff Stephanie Wilson appeared via zoom.
Defendant The Napa Deli not appearing.

The court finds good cause to continue this matter to July 11, 2025 at 9:00am as Plaintiff's witness Ms. Wilson's zoom connection was eratic and not stable.

Plaintiff is encouraged to have all parties to this action appear in person at the next hearing.

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 585(A)(&)(B)
